de.uka.ilkd.key.rule

Class SyntacticalReplaceVisitor

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.DefaultVisitor
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.SyntacticalReplaceVisitor
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Visitor
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    ConstraintAwareSyntacticalReplaceVisitor

    public class SyntacticalReplaceVisitor
extends DefaultVisitor

      this method is called in execPreOrder and execPostOrder in class Term when entering the subtree rooted in the term subtreeRoot.

      this method is called in execPreOrder and execPostOrder in class Term when leaving the subtree rooted in the term subtreeRoot.

      the method is only still invoked to allow the ConstraintAwareSyntacticalReplaceVisitor to recursively replace meta variables

      performs the syntactic replacement of schemavariables with their instantiations

      • termLabelState

      • labelHint

        protected final Taclet.TacletLabelHint labelHint

      • subStack

        private final Stack<Object> subStack
        the stack contains the subterms that will be added in the next step of execPostOrder in Term in order to build the new term. A boolean value between or under the subterms on the stack indicate that a term using these subterms should build a new term instead of using the old one, because one of its subterms has been built, too.

      • newMarker

        private final Boolean newMarker

      • tacletTermStack

        private final Deque<Term> tacletTermStack

        delivers the new built term

      • getSVInstantiations

      • subtreeEntered

        public void subtreeEntered(Term subtreeRoot)
        this method is called in execPreOrder and execPostOrder in class Term when entering the subtree rooted in the term subtreeRoot. Default implementation is to do nothing. Subclasses can override this method when the visitor behaviour depends on informations bound to subtrees.
        Specified by:
        subtreeEntered in interface Visitor
        Overrides:
        subtreeEntered in class DefaultVisitor
        Parameters:
        subtreeRoot - root of the subtree which the visitor enters.

      • subtreeLeft

        public void subtreeLeft(Term subtreeRoot)
        this method is called in execPreOrder and execPostOrder in class Term when leaving the subtree rooted in the term subtreeRoot. Default implementation is to do nothing. Subclasses can override this method when the visitor behaviour depends on informations bound to subtrees.
        Specified by:
        subtreeLeft in interface Visitor
        Overrides:
        subtreeLeft in class DefaultVisitor
        Parameters:
        subtreeRoot - root of the subtree which the visitor leaves.
